titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/135

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=405 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:03:02.617855+00 | translation moa=406 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:03:04.489623+00

p.span:
Ansel’ . de Wichetune . Uniuersis Sancte matris ecclesie filiis . tam laicis quam litteratis Salutem . Sciant tam posteri quam presentes me dedisse
et hac mea carta confirmasse deo et domui de melros et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus in perpetuam elemosinam . terram cum pratis
. . . perambulauimus . ego et Glai nepos Roberti Auenel . cum cellerario et fratribus eiusdem loci . uidelicet sicut sulcus aratri ex parte aquilonali
?tendit de uia que iacet de hunedune uersus molle et uadit usque ad rupem . et de rupe usque ad fontem . et sicut idem sulcus uadit de fon
te . . . et inde usque in burnam . et hanc elemosinam dedi eis pro anima Dauid regis . et Henrici comitis et Malcolmi regis . et pro salute
Willelmi Regis et Dauid fratis sui . et pro anima mea et uxoris mee et liberorum meorum . Preterea dedi eis in perpetuam elemosinam totam illam meam petariam
que est inter molope et berope et herdstrete que diuidit terram de molle et de hunum . Insuper dedi predictis monachis in perpetuam ele
mosinam in bosco meo de molope bushciam quantum potest unus equus portare singulis annis a pascha usque ad natiuitatem
sancte Marie . Et monachi de melros habebunt hanc meam elemosinam . et ceteras predictas et tenebunt de me et de heredibus meis quietas et
. . . seruitiis et omnibus consuetudinibus . et ego et heredes mei warantizabimus illas monachis erga omnes homines . His test
. . . abbate de neubotle . Roberto auenel . et Geruasio filio . eius . Willelmo de Sumeruilla . Iohanne filio Horm . Willelmo filio eius . Glai nepote Roberti auenel .
Gernun . Nicol’ filio ade . Petro persona de Morbotle . W . . . de Hunum .

p.cl: Anselm of Whitton sends greeting to all sons of Holy Mother Church, both lay and learned.

Let future and present know me to have given and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the monastic house of Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, in everlasting alms the land with meadows which I and Glay nephew of Robert Avenel perambulated with the cellarer and brethren of the same place, namely, as the furrow of the plough extends on the north side from the road that lies from ‘Hunedune’ towards Mow and runs down to the rock, and from the rock down to the spring, and as the same trench runs from the spring . . . and from there as far as the burn; and this alms I have given to them for the soul of King David, and of Earl Henry and King Malcolm, and for the salvation of King William and David his brother, and for my own soul and my wife’s and my childrens’ souls. In addition I have given to them in everlasting alms my whole peatery that is between Mowhope and Berhope and ‘Herdstrete’ which divides the land of Mow and of Hownam. In addition I have given to the said monks in everlasting alms in my wood of Mowhope as much firewood as one horse can carry, each year from Ester until the Nativity of Saint Mary. And the monks of Melrose shall have this my alms, and the rest aforesaid, and they shall hold of me and of my heirs quit and free from all services and all customs; and I and my heirs shall warrant those to the monks in respect of all men.
